businessDescription: Two-sided marketplace connecting locum doctors directly with hospitals and healthcare facilities. Anti-agency positioning.

brandResearch.source: App Store metadata + website analysis (2026-04-09); primary hex re-sampled from logo_blue.png on 2026-04-14

brandColors.primaryDescription: Electric royal blue, sampled directly from the StatDoctor logomark (rgb 3,62,255). This is the TRUE brand color — not muted navy. Saturated, confident, modern healthtech.
brandColors.primaryLegacyIncorrect: #284EA7 (do NOT use — this was a muted guess used in the 2026-04-13 run and was off-brand)
brandColors.accentDescription: Lighter brand blue for italic emphasis, glow blobs, secondary accents
brandColors.lightCanvasDescription: Cool off-white canvas tuned for cream/editorial layouts against the primary blue
brandColors.textDarkDescription: Deep brand-tinted navy for headlines on light canvas
brandColors.coralAccentDescription: Use sparingly for strikethrough/pain markers only. Watch for clash against saturated #033EFF — swap to white if it fights the hero blue.

typography.secondaryDescription: Friendly rounded sans-serif, softer approachable feel

logoDescription: StatDoctor wordmark, referenced as statdoc_logo.svg. Blue on white. Clean modern typography. NOTE: AI cannot perfectly replicate - for production, composite real logo asset.

creativeDirection.feel: Modern healthtech, professional but approachable. Like a doctor-built tech product. NOT generic SaaS. NOT clinical/sterile. NOT startup-purple.
creativeDirection.colorUsage: Electric royal blue #033EFF as primary brand anchor (sampled from actual logo). White for text on dark. Cool off-white (#EEF2FF) for light canvases. Never use muted navy #284EA7 (old wrong guess), and never use indigo/purple/violet.
visualCues: Medical/healthcare context without being clinical or stock-photo-y
Clean, modern app UI as hero visual
Professional but warm - these are doctors, not enterprise buyers
Trust and authority through blue tones
avoid: Indigo/purple/violet colors (looks like SaaS, not healthcare)
Dark navy backgrounds unless specifically for problem-aware drama
